steps.action: It is primarily dance music.

steps.action: ) The name indicates a dance that is accompanied
steps.text: ) The name indicates a dance that is accompanied by stomping the feet on the ground.

steps.action: There are poetic recitations accompanied by clap
steps.text: There are poetic recitations accompanied by clapping and dancing, with interludes for improvised and sometimes obscene performances on the balafon (a type of xylophone).

steps.action: During bikutsi, women dance and sing along with
steps.text: During bikutsi, women dance and sing along with the balafon, and lyrics focus on relationships, sexuality and the lives of famous people.

steps.action: These female choruses are an integral part of bi
steps.text: These female choruses are an integral part of bikutsi, and their intense dancing and screams are characteristic of the genre.

steps.action: Traditional bikutsi was often ironic in its cont
steps.text: Traditional bikutsi was often ironic in its content, as many modern bikutsi songs still are.

steps.action: International acclaim began in 1987 with the for
steps.text: International acclaim began in 1987 with the formation of Les Têtes Brûlées by Jean Marie Ahanda.

steps.action: (The balafon is a marimba-like instrument that i
steps.text: (The balafon is a marimba-like instrument that is widely used in African folk music.

steps.action: Present-day bikutsi as performed by artists like
steps.text: Present-day bikutsi as performed by artists like Lady Ponce, K-Tino, Racine Sagath and Natascha Bizo is sometimes regarded as controversial.

steps.action: It has been criticised for the perceived sexual
steps.text: It has been criticised for the perceived sexual content of its lyrics and dancing style.

steps.action: The main difference is that present day bikutsi
steps.text: The main difference is that present day bikutsi is still often performed by female artists who use it as a means of self-expression in a traditionally male-dominated society.

steps.action: Among the current crop of artists are Patou Bass
steps.text: Among the current crop of artists are Patou Bass and Ovasho Bens, the promoter of a dance and philosophy called "zig zag".

steps.action: His first album is composed not only of Cameroon
steps.text: His first album is composed not only of Cameroonian traditional rhythms but also West Indian zouk and Jamaican-style reggae.

steps.action: Bikutsi has influenced Western musicians such as
steps.text: Bikutsi has influenced Western musicians such as Paul Simon on his 1990 album The Rhythm of the Saints.

steps.action: Bikutsi is a musical genre from Cameroon.
steps.text: Bikutsi is a musical genre from Cameroon.

steps.action: It was popular in the middle of the 20th century
steps.text: It was popular in the middle of the 20th century in West Africa.

steps.action: it is played at all sorts of Beti gatherings, in
steps.text: it is played at all sorts of Beti gatherings, including parties, funerals and weddings.

steps.action: The Ekang phase is intensely musical, and usuall
steps.text: The Ekang phase is intensely musical, and usually lasts all night.

steps.action: These interludes signal the shift to the bikutsi
steps.text: These interludes signal the shift to the bikutsi phase, which is much less strictly structured than Ekang.

steps.action: In its modern form, bikutsi is very popular, and
steps.text: In its modern form, bikutsi is very popular, and rivals makossa as the country's most renowned style.

steps.action: Some twenty years later, the style was electrifi
steps.text: Some twenty years later, the style was electrified with the addition of keyboards and guitars.

steps.action: The most popular performer of this period was Me
steps.text: The most popular performer of this period was Messi Me Nkonda Martin, frontman for Los Camaroes and known as "the father of modern bikutsi music".

steps.action: Played in this way, the electric guitar sounded
steps.text: Played in this way, the electric guitar sounded similar to the balafon.
